mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2009 payroll employment at 131.5M, down from pre-crisis peaks
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 20d forward returns exceeds 0.05 in magnitude, or negative payroll months produce positive forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, further easing is constrained and equity valuations become sensitive to real economic growth signals rather than discount rate compression, reversing the typical easing-positive relationship and producing negative returns when growth remains weak.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% AND GDPC1 growth rate < 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 0.16% in July 2009, near zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary regime with weak growth
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth expectations. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings upward in response to near-term demand signals.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at $342.3B in July 2009, recovering from crisis lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month percentage change and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 in magnitude, or positive retail sales months produce negative forward returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines, it signals supply-side weakness and falling corporate profitability. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production at 95.97 in July 2009, still below pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC is < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector demand. When both rise together, it indicates broad-based demand recovery that supports equity valuations. The interaction captures the multiplier effect of coordinated demand expansion, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts at 582K in June 2009, retail sales recovering
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 9% signals severe labor market slack that constrains consumer spending and corporate hiring expectations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 9.4% in July 2009, elevated post-crisis level
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 9%, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and forward returns is < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) declining or remaining subdued signals deflationary pressure or low inflation expectations. In a low-rate environment, this reduces real discount rates further and supports equity valuations through lower required returns, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI at 214.47 in July 2009, low inflation environment with near-zero rates
regime_hint: low inflation, low rates monetary regime
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC is < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth acceleration signals strengthening economic fundamentals and corporate earnings expansion. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices long-term profit expectations upward in response to sustained demand growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP at $12,892.4B in Q2 2009, beginning recovery from recession
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized, 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and forward returns is < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
